reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Code reading is accurate. The cache key at gossipHandlers.ts:296 is blockRootHex (computed from unsigned header), and hasColumn() check at line 301 only includes column index, not signature. Cache hit at line 301 triggers early return at line 307 BEFORE validateGossipDataColumnSidecar (line 317), which contains signature verification (dataColumnSidecar.ts:127-137). An attacker can send a valid DataColumnSidecar to populate cache, then resend with forged signature but same header to bypass validation. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-032 (file outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json, lines 824-841) requires 'verify_signature(item.sig) must still execute on new instance' - code clearly violates this. Severity: Original HIGH downgraded to MEDIUM because this is a single-client vulnerability (Lodestar only per TARGET_INFO.json) and lacks evidence of >33% network market share (BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json severity thresholds). An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip (untrusted entry point) by sending valid DataColumnSidecar followed by resubmission with forged signature, causing honest nodes to accept and rebroadcast invalid-signature sidecars to peers.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Dedup cache hit must not bypass signature verification on resubmitted items with different proofs. (forall item, seen_set: item.id in seen_set => verify_signature(item.sig) must still execute on new instance)
